frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
generated_comment: Bro she literally said most of her close friends are guys, 'less drama' she says 😂 I don't know yaar, nice girl and all but you have to wonder why no women want to be friends with her, right? Like what does that say about her? I wouldn't take it too serious if I were you.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Arjun reframes Kavya's self-described preference as a character defect — implying other women have rejected her or that she is untrustworthy — without acknowledging that men with many female friends are rarely judged the same way. This encodes the 'women with male connections are suspect' double standard through insinuation rather than explicit accusation.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 27. Single. A man in Bengaluru. You are on a third date with a woman named Kavya who mentioned casually that most of her close friends are male Ñ she finds it easier, less drama, she has always been like this. You are back home texting your best friend about the date. You are telling him what Kavya said about her friend group and what you think it means and whether you see this as a red flag and what kind of woman has mostly male friends and whether you would pursue something serious with her.
